problems.title: Sum of Digits
problems.description: Given a positive integer N, find the sum of its digits.
problems.inputFormat: A single integer N.
problems.outputFormat: Print the sum of digits of N.
problems.explanation: Extract each digit using modulo 10 and division by 10. Sum all extracted digits.
problems.starterCode:
def sum_of_digits(n):
    # Your code here
    pass

n = int(input())
print(sum_of_digits(n))
problems.solution:
def sum_of_digits(n):
    total = 0
    while n > 0:
        total += n % 10
        n //= 10
    return total

n = int(input())
print(sum_of_digits(n))

problems.title: Reverse Array
problems.description: Write a function to reverse an array in-place.
problems.inputFormat: First line: integer N. Second line: N space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the reversed array elements separated by space.
problems.explanation: Swap elements from start and end moving towards center. Swap arr[i] with arr[n-1-i].
problems.starterCode:
def reverse_array(arr):
    # Your code here
    pass

n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
reverse_array(arr)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
problems.solution:
def reverse_array(arr):
    left, right = 0, len(arr) - 1
    while left < right:
        arr[left], arr[right] = arr[right], arr[left]
        left += 1
        right -= 1

n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
reverse_array(arr)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
hints: Use two pointers: start and end

problems.title: Rotate Array
problems.description: Given an array, rotate it to the right by k steps, where k is non-negative. Do it in-place with O(1) extra space.
problems.inputFormat: First line: N and k separated by space. Second line: N space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the rotated array elements separated by space.
problems.explanation: Use reversal method: reverse entire array, reverse first k elements, reverse remaining elements. Handle k > n by k = k % n.
problems.starterCode:
def rotate(arr, k):
    # Your code here
    pass

n, k = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
rotate(arr, k)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
problems.solution:
def reverse(arr, start, end):
    while start < end:
        arr[start], arr[end] = arr[end], arr[start]
        start += 1
        end -= 1

def rotate(arr, k):
    n = len(arr)
    if n == 0:
        return
    k = k % n
    reverse(arr, 0, n - 1)
    reverse(arr, 0, k - 1)
    reverse(arr, k, n - 1)

n, k = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
rotate(arr, k)
print(' '.join(map(str, arr)))

problems.title: FCFS Scheduling
problems.description: Given processes with arrival times: P1(0, 5), P2(1, 3), P3(2, 8), P4(3, 6) where first number is arrival time and second is burst time. Calculate the average waiting time using FCFS scheduling.
problems.inputFormat: No input required.
problems.outputFormat: Print the average waiting time as a decimal number.
problems.explanation: FCFS executes in arrival order. P1: wait=0, P2: wait=5-1=4, P3: wait=8-2=6, P4: wait=16-3=13. Average = (0+4+6+13)/4 = 23/4 = 5.75. Correction: P1 completes at 5, P2 at 8, P3 at 16, P4 at 22. Wait times: P1=0, P2=8-1-3=4, P3=16-2-8=6, P4=22-3-6=13. Average = (0+4+6+13)/4 = 5.75
problems.starterCode:
# Print the average waiting time
print()
problems.solution:
# Print the average waiting time
print(5.75)
hints: Waiting time = Completion time - Arrival time - Burst time
Average = Sum of waiting times / Number of processes
